## [00:00 - 03:02]

Let's talk for a second about mindset. I talk to hundreds of business owners every single week. They vary from being a practice owner all the way to being an entrepreneur with a billion dollar business. If you ever want to understand any human being at any level, this is business, personal, professional, it doesn't matter. When you start a dialogue with them, listen to the kind of answers they give you to your questions. The kinds of answers they give you will give you full transparency into their current mindset. You just need to listen and ask the right questions. When I run across anybody and I ask, hey, how's your business doing? And they respond, oh, I'm struggling. My competitors are beating me. This person is undercutting my prices. These employees are hard to deal with. Ever comes out of their mouth? I know if it's negative, their mindset is wrong. If they never want to talk about themselves and they only talk about their struggles, then they're projecting because they're struggling inside. Their belief in themselves and their business is very, very low. This is an indicator for you to pay attention. When you're sitting across the table from somebody and they're looking you straight in the eye and you're like, how's it going? And they're like, man, it's going good. I'm going to get bigger. We're crushing it. We're doing this. We're doing that. It tells you that they're thinking about growth. And to be honest with you, that takes self-reflection. What am I doing well? What can I do better? And how do I get bigger? That's what they'll talk about. There is an obvious difference between projecting away from yourself to spending time reflecting on yourself. Projecting away from yourself is the easiest way out, right? It's somebody or something else's fault. Projecting on yourself and taking responsibilities takes a lot of diligence and emotional intelligence. You have to be strong and fortified in your mind. So I'll ask you now, how's your career doing currently? Why do you want to acquire a cash flowing business? There's an assignment in the resource section that will take you through this process. And to be honest with yourself, are you focused on others and what they're doing? Are you projecting or are you looking inward and self-reflecting on how you can do better and have a bigger impact on your personal, professional and financial life through a business acquisition? What is your reason why? Having a powerful and personal reason why is the only way you'll follow through right now wanting something bigger. So go to the resource section of this module and download the acquisition mindset document and complete the assignment. When you're finished, I'll see you in the next lesson. There's one more quick topic I want to cover before we take the mindset work that we've
